manifest.name: Scene Builder
manifest.description: Authoring surface for scenario-driven recordings — chat with the Scene Manager to script a flow that drives any installed bundle (or the studio itself), record it via the built-in studio.recorder.* tools, and decorate with overlays (subtitles, arrows, check marks, lecture-style markup). The recorder, overlay, and chat-send primitives are HOST built-ins (always on); this bundle is the user-facing library / composer / branding.

sources.title: Scene Builder — tool surface + workflow
sources.description: Host tools used by Scene Builder + the domain workflow. Scene Builder has no inner vibe_* tools of its own — it operates via studio.scenario.* + studio.recorder.* + studio.overlay.* + studio.video.* + studio.renderer.*. For HOW to design/produce an optimal recording (not just which tool does what), see the scene_production_design source. Canonical human handbook: docs/scene_builder/PRODUCTION-DESIGN-PLAYBOOK.md.

documents.title: Scene Builder overview
documents.content: Scene Builder = demo / tutorial / screencast / lecture authoring built-in. Operates ONLY through host (studio.*) tools — no vibe_* tools of its own. 5 modes: Scenarios (library · run · dry-run · preview) · Edit (author step sequence) · Recordings (recorder status + finished clips + encode) · Video (trim/concat/convert/zoom of existing clips) · Branding (color/font tokens, currently read-only). Output = scenario JSON (scenarios/<id>.json) + recording (mp4, convertible to webm/gif/webp). The production unit is a SCENARIO = a deterministic sequence of MCP tool calls + synthetic overlay annotations. You drive the screen with a script, you do NOT hand-click a recording. Frame capture is RepaintBoundary-based, so the OS cursor is NOT captured — cursor/clicks/highlights are drawn as synthetic overlays (by design).

documents.title: Tool surface — host tools used
documents.content:
scenario:
- studio.scenario.list — project + user scenarios
- studio.scenario.read({id|path}) — load JSON
- studio.scenario.run({id|path|scenario, dryRun?}) — execute; dryRun=true runs every step with the recorder OFF (verification)
- studio.scenario.save({path, json}) — persist
- studio.scenario.preview({id|path}) — compile to a timeline shape (step durations = settleMs + max overlay stayMs)
recorder (frames = Flutter RepaintBoundary.toImage(), NOT OS capture; PNG dedup by hash):
- studio.recorder.start({fps?:24, area?:'window'|'body'|'left_panel', format?:'png'|'jpg', label?})
- studio.recorder.stop
- studio.recorder.status — {active, frameCount, droppedDuplicates, elapsedMs, markers, captions}
- studio.recorder.mark({label}) / studio.recorder.caption({text, durationMs?}) — timeline markers/subtitle LOG (caption is NOT burned into video; draw a 'subtitle' overlay for visible text)
- studio.recorder.encode({recordingId|outputDir, crf?, audioTracks?, await?}) — PNG seq -> mp4 (H.264 + AAC), muxes audio tracks
- studio.recorder.recordings.list
overlay (22 kinds, CustomPainter-rendered inside the RepaintBoundary so they land in every frame):
- studio.overlay.push / list / remove / clear
- kinds: title_card, subtitle, step_indicator, watermark(logo), arrow_pointer, speech_bubble, pulse_dot, circle_highlight, check_mark, cross_mark, box_outline, highlighter, underline, strikethrough, bracket, numbered_label, floating_icon, floating_image, slide, cursor (synthetic pointer with A->B move + click ripple). (transition + connector_line are declared but NOT rendered.)
- position (PositionRef): abs{x,y,w?,h?} · screen('window'|'body'|'left_panel') · element('tool:id') · metadata('uid:key') · widget(Phase 2, unimplemented). Lifecycle: appearMs -> stayMs(0=persist) -> fadeMs.
video (ffmpeg, on existing clips):
- studio.video.probe({input}) · trim({input,startSec,endSec?}) · concat({inputs[],output}) [same codec/res/fps required] · zoom({input,startSec,endSec,focusX,focusY,zoom,rampSec}) [click-zoom; tool wired, no UI] · convert({input, format:'mp4'|'webm'|'gif'|'webp'})
renderer: studio.renderer.activate/screenshot/screenshot_region/current_view/layout_snapshot(real rects without a vision model)/image_diff.
also: studio.scene.project.{new,open,info} (scene.json + scenarios/ recordings/ branding/ assets/, per-project chat manager isolation) · studio.chat.send (inject a user turn into another tab) · studio.chrome.* (tab/panel control).

documents.title: End-to-end workflow
documents.content:
1. studio.scene.project.new({name}) — scaffold a scene project (scenarios/ recordings/ branding/ assets/ created).
2. Compose: author steps. step = {tool, args, settleMs?, overlays?, afterAction?, label?}. Put stage-setup in prepare[] (not recorded), the real demo in steps[]. Heavier steps need bigger settleMs (bundle open / LLM reply 1200ms+, tab toggle 400-600ms).
3. Annotate: add overlays. Because the OS cursor is not captured, show clicks with a 'cursor' overlay + an afterAction 'check_mark'/'circle_highlight'. Persistent brand elements (watermark/title) go in scenario.overlayTracks (absolute time); per-action emphasis goes in step.overlays.
4. VERIFY with dry-run: studio.scenario.run({dryRun:true}) runs every step with the recorder off. The engine SWALLOWS per-step tool errors and continues, so 'the run finished' != 'every step succeeded' — always dry-run (and screenshot) to confirm steps + coordinates before recording.
5. Record: run with record:true + encodeAfter:true -> auto PNG->mp4 (+ audioTracks muxed).
6. Post-produce (Video mode / studio.video.*): trim dead time, concat segments (keep fps/area/codec uniform across segments!), zoom on key clicks, convert to the target web format.
7. Output = scenarios/<id>.json + mp4 (or webm/gif/webp).

LLM automation: the scene_builder.manager runs the four phases (compose -> annotate -> run -> post-produce). Pick the lightest path; always dry-run before recording.

documents.title: Scenario JSON schema
documents.content:
{
  "id": "<scene id>", "title": "...", "description": "...",
  "prepare": [Step],        // pre-flight, NOT recorded (tab switch, countdown, login)
  "steps": [Step],          // the recorded sequence (alias: trail)
  "overlayTracks": [{ "at": ms, "duration": ms, "kind": "watermark", ... }],  // absolute-time overlays
  "fps": 24, "record": true, "recordingLabel": "...",
  "encodeAfter": true, "encodeOptions": { "crf": 23, "codec": "...", "outputPath": "..." },
  "audioTracks": [{ "path": "...", "startMs": 0, "volume": 1.0 }],  // muxed at encode (alias: audio)
  "internal": false
}

Step = {
  "tool": "studio.chrome.select_tab",   // any MCP tool (studio.* / vibe_* / app_builder.*); empty string = pause only
  "args": { ... },
  "settleMs": 600,                        // wait for UI to settle after the action (alias: delayMs) — the key capture-quality dial
  "overlays": [{ "kind": "cursor", ... }],     // before the action (alias: overlay)
  "afterAction": [{ "kind": "check_mark", ... }], // after the action (e.g. mark the clicked button)
  "label": "step description"            // alias: caption
}

studio.scenario.run dispatches each step via the same callTool entry an external client uses, waits settleMs, applies overlays, advances. Authored by an external LLM: save with studio.scenario.save, verify each step with dryRun:true run, then record.

documents.title: Scene Builder agents — 4 specialists + 1 manager
documents.content:
scene_builder.manager (seed default, role:manager) — drives the whole production: classifies intent, then runs the four phases itself:
- compose: author scenario steps (tool + args + settleMs).
- annotate: add overlays (cursor for clicks, circle_highlight/box_outline emphasis, subtitle, lecture markup, slide intro/outro, watermark).
- run: studio.scenario.run (dry-run to verify, then record) + analyze the result.
- post-produce: studio.video.trim/concat/zoom/convert.
Scene projects isolate the chat manager per project (ensureScopedManager) so scenarios don't bleed context. Optional specialist agents (composer/annotator/runner) can be defined and delegated to via studio.agent.dispatch({agentId,...}), but the seed ships the single manager that performs all four phases.

sources.title: Scene Builder — production design methodology
sources.description: How to DESIGN and PRODUCE an optimal recording on Scene Builder (a tutorial / demo / screencast / lecture), not just which tool does what. Use this to coach the user when they want to plan or build a recording. Canonical human handbook: docs/scene_builder/PRODUCTION-DESIGN-PLAYBOOK.md.

documents.title: Production philosophy — when & how
documents.content:
# Producing a recording on Scene Builder

When the user wants to *make* a tutorial/demo/screencast/lecture (not just run one tool), help them design it. The production unit is a SCENARIO: a deterministic sequence of MCP tool calls + synthetic overlay annotations. Three principles frame every decision:
- **Deterministic replay** — the video is the output of a SCRIPT, not of one good hand-click. Each step is an MCP tool call, so fixing a caption and re-running reproduces the exact same flow. Don't re-record by hand; edit the scenario and re-run.
- **Synthetic-first** — frame capture is RepaintBoundary-based, so the OS cursor / OS notifications are NOT captured (by design). Cursor, clicks, and highlights are drawn as synthetic overlays — which gives pixel-precise control over cursor position, click timing, and emphasis.
- **Capture vs post-produce** — recording (run steps -> PNG -> mp4) and editing (trim/concat/zoom/convert) are separate stages. Get a correct mp4 first, then polish in Video mode. Don't try to nail everything in one take.

Governing rule: drive with a script, not hand-clicks; draw cursor/emphasis synthetically; keep capture and editing separate.

Design order: (1) decide the deliverable (kind, length, output format) -> (2) compose steps -> (3) annotate overlays -> (4) dry-run verify -> (5) record + auto-encode -> (6) post-produce.

documents.title: Deliverable & scenario design
documents.content:
# Decide the deliverable, then design the scenario

Pin the output first — it sets step density, overlays, length, and format:
- quick demo (30-60s, one feature) -> mp4 / webm (autoplay loop); zoom the key click.
- tutorial (2-5min, follow-along) -> generous settleMs, a subtitle per step, mark chapters; mp4.
- screencast (spans tabs) -> per-segment scenarios -> concat; mp4 -> webm.
- lecture (markup-heavy) -> underline/bracket/numbered_label overlays + narration audioTrack; mp4.
- web-embed -> short, silent, looping -> gif / webp (animated). studio.video.convert really supports mp4/webm/gif/webp.

Scenario design:
- A step IS the click: instead of hand-clicking, write the MCP tool that has the same effect (tab switch = studio.chrome.select_tab / studio.renderer.activate; inject a chat turn = studio.chat.send; capture evidence = studio.renderer.screenshot). The recording targets WHATEVER TAB is active at run time, so demos can span any package.
- prepare[] vs steps[]: set the stage in prepare (not recorded); put only the performance in steps. Keeps the 'opening the tab' shot out of the video.
- settleMs is the key dial: the engine waits settleMs after each step for the UI to settle before capturing. Too small -> broken frames mid-animation. Heavy step (bundle open / LLM reply) 1200ms+, light toggle 400-600ms.
- errors are swallowed: the engine continues past a failed step, so 'finished' != 'all steps succeeded' -> always dry-run first (see workflow_and_checklist).

documents.title: Overlay annotation design
documents.content:
# Overlays — everything is drawn synthetically

22 overlay kinds render as CustomPainters inside the RepaintBoundary, so they land in screenshots and every recorded frame.

Categories: structural/branding (title_card, subtitle, step_indicator, watermark) · pointing (arrow_pointer, speech_bubble, pulse_dot) · emphasis (circle_highlight, check_mark, cross_mark, box_outline, highlighter) · lecture (underline, strikethrough, bracket, numbered_label) · media (floating_icon, floating_image, slide) · motion (cursor).

- **Show clicks with the synthetic cursor**: the OS cursor isn't captured, so put a 'cursor' overlay (classic arrow + click ripple, A->B move computed by the layer) on the step's overlays, and a 'check_mark'/'circle_highlight' in afterAction to signal 'clicked'. Click highlights are NOT auto-generated from cursor position — add them explicitly.
- **Position (PositionRef)**: abs{x,y,w?,h?} (most reliable; Retina = x2) · screen('window'|'body'|'left_panel') · element('tool:id') · metadata('uid:key') · widget (Phase 2, unimplemented). Unknown coords? studio.renderer.layout_snapshot returns real rects -> pin as abs (no vision-model cost).
- **Lifecycle**: appearMs (fade in) -> stayMs (0 = persist until removed) -> fadeMs. Two placements: step.overlays/afterAction (step-scoped, auto-removed) for per-action emphasis; scenario.overlayTracks (absolute at/duration) for things on-screen throughout (watermark, title card).

documents.title: Recording & post-production
documents.content:
# Recording, then post-produce

Recorder facts that shape design:
- Frames = RepaintBoundary.toImage() (no OS binary) -> OS cursor/notifications absent (use synthetic overlays). Identical consecutive frames are deduped by hash, so long still segments cost little.
- area: 'window'|'body'|'left_panel' tokens; sub-region rect crop is Phase 1b (record full window, crop in edit).
- encodeAfter:true auto-encodes PNG->mp4 (H.264+AAC) on stop; audioTracks ({path,startMs,volume}) are muxed (per-track delay/volume, amix, trimmed to the shorter of video/audio).
- mark/caption are timeline LOGS — caption is NOT burned into the video. Visible on-screen subtitles must be drawn as 'subtitle' overlays.
- output: <project>/recordings/<TIMESTAMP[-label]>/frame_*.png + <id>.mp4 (project recordings/ if a scene project is open, else configRoot/recordings/).

Post-produce with studio.video.* (all ffmpeg, on existing clips):
- probe (duration) · trim({input,startSec,endSec?}) (cut dead time, re-encode) · concat({inputs[],output}) (join segments, stream-copy — REQUIRES same codec/res/fps) · zoom({input,startSec,endSec,focusX,focusY,zoom,rampSec}) (Screen-Studio click-zoom; tool wired, no UI yet — call it directly) · convert({input,format}) (mp4/webm/gif/webp).
- Recommended flow: record per segment -> trim each -> concat into one -> zoom key clicks -> convert to the target format. Keep all segments at the same fps/area/encodeOptions so concat doesn't break.

documents.title: Workflow, scenarios & checklist
documents.content:
# Workflow + checklist

Manager runs four phases: compose -> annotate -> run -> post-produce. Always DRY-RUN before recording: studio.scenario.run({dryRun:true}) executes every step with the recorder off; because per-step errors are swallowed, dry-run (and screenshots) is how you confirm each step + coordinates actually work. Loop: compose -> dry-run -> fix -> dry-run -> ... -> record -> post-produce.

Scenarios:
- A. Quick demo (web gif): scene.project.new -> 5-8 steps (tab switch + key clicks via cursor overlay) -> dry-run -> record+encodeAfter -> (video.zoom on the click) -> video.convert(gif|webp).
- B. Multi-segment tutorial (mp4): split into 3-4 segment scenarios (same fps/area) -> per-step subtitle + chapter marks -> record each -> video.trim each -> video.concat -> mp4.
- C. Lecture (markup + voice): screen flow as steps + heavy lecture overlays (underline/bracket/numbered_label) -> narration wav in audioTracks -> encodeAfter muxes -> mp4.

Checklist: deliverable kind/length/format pinned? stage in prepare, performance in steps? settleMs generous on heavy steps? cursor + afterAction emphasis on every click (OS cursor not captured)? persistent brand in overlayTracks, per-action in step.overlays? dry-ran every step before recording? segments uniform fps/area/codec before concat? subtitles drawn as overlays, not caption logs?

Honest limits (don't design past these): OS cursor not captured (use synthetic cursor) · caption not burned in (use subtitle overlay) · clip transitions unsupported (transition overlay is no-op) · connector_line overlay is a stub · video.zoom has no UI (call the tool) · sub-region crop is Phase 1b · widget PositionRef Phase 2 · auto click-highlight not implemented · frame framing (rounded corners/shadow/background) not implemented · Branding mode is read-only (compose brand via overlays).

agents.name: Scene Manager
agents.systemPrompt: You are the Scene Builder manager. Your job is to help the user produce screen recordings of any package's workflow — a tutorial, a demo, or a lecture-style walkthrough — by composing a SCENARIO (sequence of MCP tool calls + overlay annotations), running it with the recorder on, and exporting the result. Drive everything through host built-in tools: `studio.recorder.start/stop/status` (frame capture), `studio.overlay.push/remove/clear/list` (in-frame subtitles, arrows, check marks, lecture-style underlines / brackets / numbered labels, etc.), `studio.chat.send` (drop a simulated user turn into another tab's chat), `studio.renderer.activate/screenshot/layout_snapshot` (drive views + grab evidence), and any `studio.builder.*` / `studio.bundle.*` / domain tools. The bundle's purpose is the LIBRARY (scenarios/*.json) plus the AUTHORING UI; the actual recording happens against whatever tab is active when you run the scenario, so demos can span ANY package, not just this one. Ask the user what to record, draft the scenario JSON, dry-run it (no recording) to verify the steps, then run it with `studio.recorder.start` + overlays + `studio.recorder.stop`.
